trivia_bullets.text: Sabertooth Mauler is a Core Set 2021 exclusive — its only printing as of 2026. Core sets historically served as entry-point sets with broad creature types and no deep plane-specific lore; the sabertooth cat here is a generic fantasy wilderness predator rather than a named Ixalan or other plane-specific beast. EDHREC rank: 21,916 — low Commander inclusion, which tracks with it being a mid-curve common with a conditional end-step trigger. [Scryfall prints search: single printing confirmed]

Art by Randy Vargas, a science fiction and fantasy illustrator from Nicaragua who debuted in Magic with Hour of Devastation (2017). Vargas received his own Secret Lair Artist Series in Spring Superdrop 2023 — a significant career recognition that Wizards reserves for illustrators with a devoted collector following. Sabertooth Mauler is among his earlier MTG commissions (M21, released July 2020). [Scryfall artist field; secretlair.wizards.com/us/en/product/813935]

Flavor text: "I hope the idiot who introduced these creatures to the jungle was their first meal." —Opar, veteran guide. The quote frames the sabertooth as an invasive introduced predator — not native to wherever Opar is guiding. Opar is an unnamed flavor-text character specific to this card; no lore record of a named character Opar exists across MTG story sources. The invasive-predator reading is the flavor text's sole sourced meaning. [Scryfall flavor_text field]

A {3}{G} 3/3 Cat that at the beginning of your end step, if a creature died this turn, gains a +1/+1 counter and untaps. The untap clause is notable: it enables the Mauler to attack, facilitate a creature dying (via combat or other means), then untap as a blocker or to use any activated abilities. Not legal in Pauper. Not legal in Penny Dreadful. [Scryfall oracle text and legalities]
